question: Is the original book cover replaced with the fantasy forest illustration (featuring mushrooms and flora) from the second input image?
Are the title '森之秘语' and subtitle '奇幻植物与生灵图鉴' clearly visible and correctly positioned on the new cover?
Does the title text exhibit a gold foil stamping texture similar to the third input image, rather than a flat color fill?
Do the metallic highlights on the gold title text align with the light source coming from the upper left (e.g., brighter on top/left edges)?
Does the new cover design accurately follow the physical curvature, perspective, and bending of the book as seen in the original image?
Are the fingers holding the book properly visible on top of the new cover design (correct occlusion), without the design appearing over the skin?
Is the boundary between the fingers and the book cover clean and realistic, showing contact shadows/pressure rather than jagged cutouts?
Is the original diagonal light reflection/highlight band preserved and visible on top of the new cover design?
Does the new cover surface show subtle printing grain or paper texture, avoiding a perfectly smooth or digital appearance?
Is there visible slight wear or weathering on the edges of the book cover, consistent with the edit instruction?
Does the sharpness and depth of field of the new cover match the focal plane of the original photograph (not overly sharp or blurry)?
Is the background (park bench, grass, trees) completely unaltered?
Are the hands and fingers preserved with their original skin tone and anatomy (no extra digits or distortions)?
Is the image free from visual artifacts such as ghosting, color bleeding, or obvious composition seams?
